issues: generic treasure-chest silhouette risks dungeon-crawler read
pixel rendering is competent but lacks extra whimsy sparkle expected of Hyrule/fairy-tale register
face expression is neutral rather than expressive/charming
reasoning: Candidate (right): warm orange-brown wooden box with a small pixel face. Warm hues and anthropomorphised face point in the right direction—not grimdark, not corporate, not fan-service. However the overall read is closer to 'generic RPG loot chest' than the toy-diorama, sunlit-Hyrule target. The face is present but understated, missing the bubbly expressiveness and magical sparkle that push an icon firmly into Nintendo-family-friendly whimsy. Peer references (left) are gray dungeon-crawler helmets; by contrast the candidate is warmer and friendlier, but that bar is low. No out-of-tone signals (no darkness, no realism, no occult cues), so a hard fail is unwarranted, but the charm ceiling isn't fully reached.
